steps.title: Identify the subject agreement
steps.content:
The phrase 'After much debate' is a prepositional phrase describing a group acting on something.

The blank introduces the subject of the main clause 'made a decision.' The subsequent phrase 'the judges for the 1947 Nobel Prize in Literature' explicitly identifies the decision-makers as a plural group.

steps.title: Match plural agreement and context
steps.content:
The correct choice must be a plural pronoun referring to the judges, a collective noun. 'They' (C) matches both the grammatical requirement for plural agreement and the context of a group making a decision.

The other choices either mismatch number (A and D are singular; B is either singular or ambiguous) or fail to align with the explicit subject 'judges.' That matches choice C — C is correct.
